title: Agents and tools
text: Agents can also driven raw text token ids.
code:
library(Rbebelm) model <- bebel_model_load(Sys.getenv("BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE"), num_threads = 2) agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 48, max_think = 16)  bebel_append_user(agent, "Say exactly: Paris noted.") turn1 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  bebel_append_user(agent, "Say exactly: second turn complete.") turn2 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  bebel_agent_info(agent)[c("history_tokens", "processed_tokens", "kv_tokens")] #> $history_tokens #> [1] 82 #>  #> $processed_tokens #> [1] 80 #>  #> $kv_tokens #> [1] 80  # Direct methods are available on the agent object. length(agent$history()) #> [1] 82 substr(agent$transcript(), 1, 80) #> [1] "<|startoftext|><|im_start|>user
Say exactly: Paris noted.<|im_end|>
<|im_start|>"  # Helper functions provide the same operations. length(bebel_history(agent)) #> [1] 82 substr(bebel_transcript(agent), 1, 80) #> [1] "<|startoftext|><|im_start|>user
Say exactly: Paris noted.<|im_end|>
<|im_start|>"  # Reset the conversation while keeping the loaded weights and generation settings. agent$clear()[c("history_tokens", "processed_tokens", "kv_tokens")] #> $history_tokens #> [1] 0 #>  #> $processed_tokens #> [1] 0 #>  #> $kv_tokens #> [1] 0 raw_agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 16, max_think = 0) bebel_append(raw_agent, "The capital of France is") raw_turn <- bebel_agent_generate(raw_agent, on_event = NULL) raw_turn[c("stop", "generated_tokens")] #> $stop #> [1] "max_new" #>  #> $generated_tokens #> [1] 16  ids <- bebel_tokenize(model, " and Italy is", add_bos = FALSE) bebel_append_tokens(raw_agent, ids) bebel_history(raw_agent)[1:8] #> [1] 124894    597   5205    302   3980    355   4741     22

title: Agents and tools
text: tool R function plus metadata. context environment private R appended model transcript. default parser accepts JSON calls, simple function calls, BebeLM’s bracketed tool-call form.
code: library(Rbebelm) ctx <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) ctx$thread_id <- "thread-001" ctx$log <- character()  tools <- list(   lookup_capital = bebel_tool(     "lookup_capital",     function(args, context, call) {       context$log <- c(context$log, paste("tool", call$name, args$country))       c(France = "Paris", Italy = "Rome")[[args$country]]     },     description = "Return a capital city for a country."   ) )  tools$lookup_capital #> <bebelTool> lookup_capital  #>   Return a capital city for a country. bebel_parse_tool_call('{"name":"lookup_capital","arguments":{"country":"Italy"}}') #> $name #> [1] "lookup_capital" #>  #> $arguments #> $arguments$country #> [1] "Italy" #>  #>  #> $raw #> [1] "{\"name\":\"lookup_capital\",\"arguments\":{\"country\":\"Italy\"}}" bebel_parse_tool_call('lookup_capital({"country":"Italy"})') #> $name #> [1] "lookup_capital" #>  #> $arguments #> $arguments$country #> [1] "Italy" #>  #>  #> $raw #> [1] "lookup_capital({\"country\":\"Italy\"})" bebel_parse_tool_call('[lookup_capital(country="Italy")]') #> $name #> [1] "lookup_capital" #>  #> $arguments #> $arguments$country #> [1] "Italy" #>  #>  #> $raw #> [1] "[lookup_capital(country=\"Italy\")]"

title: Agents and tools
text: bebel_agent_run() dispatches tools generation emits BebeLM tool_call_end event. prompt asks directly tool-call form example exercises dispatch path. model uses different tool-call format, pass custom parse_tool_call function bebel_agent_run().
code: hooks <- list(   tool_request = function(call, context, ...) {     context$log <- c(context$log, paste("request", call$name))   },   tool_result = function(call, result, context, ...) {     context$log <- c(context$log, paste("result", call$name, result))   } )  tool_prompt <- paste(   "Return exactly this tool call and no other text:",   "lookup_capital({\"country\":\"Italy\"})" )  agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 64, max_think = 0) bebel_append_user(agent, tool_prompt) run <- bebel_agent_run(agent, tools = tools, context = ctx, hooks = hooks, max_steps = 2)  length(run$tool_calls) #> [1] 1 ctx$log #> [1] "request lookup_capital"     "tool lookup_capital Italy"  #> [3] "result lookup_capital Rome"

title: Backends and webR
text: Backend loading happens per R process. want request backend, call rbebelm_set_backend() first call loads backend symbols. Supported backend names depend platform. Typical native builds include: scalar avx2 avx512 x86_64 built neon arm64 built requested backend installed supported current CPU, dispatcher reports error model code loaded.
code: rbebelm_set_backend("auto") #> Error in `rbebelm_set_backend()`: #> ! Rbebelm backend is already initialized; call rbebelm_set_backend() before loading a model or querying backend features rbebelm_backend_features() #> <Rbebelm backend features> #>   backend: avx2  #>   target: x86_64-linux  #>   Rust crate: rbebelm_backend 0.1.0  #>   native SIMD feature: yes  #>   compiled features: #>     AVX2: yes  #>     AVX-512F: no  #>     NEON: no  #>     wasm simd128: no rbebelm_backend_info() #> <Rbebelm backend dispatch> #>   mode: dynamic  #>   requested: auto  #>   selected: avx2  #>   loaded: yes  #>   installed: scalar,avx2,avx512  #>   supported: scalar,avx2

title: Backends and webR
text: webR build links static wasm_simd128 Rust backend. uses patched local copy upstream BebeLM Emscripten: GGUF files read webR filesystem memory instead using native mmap. Matmul runs serially native Rayon threading used webR. bebel_model_load() attempts load GGUF path webR virtual filesystem. large GGUF files can exhaust browser webR memory. Use smaller models browser/runtime settings appropriate target deployment. webR diagnostics report static backend:
code: rbebelm_backend_info() rbebelm_backend_features()

title: Events and adapters
text: Use on_event = NULL silent generation, provide callback consume selected events.
code: model <- bebel_model_load(Sys.getenv("BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE"), num_threads = 2) text <- character() thinking <- character()  turn <- bebel_generate(   model,   "A text delta callback can",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 12,   max_think = 16,   on_event = bebel_event_handler(     text_delta = function(event) text <<- c(text, event$delta),     thinking_delta = function(event) thinking <<- c(thinking, event$delta)   ) )  paste0(text, collapse = "") #> [1] " be used to update a text field in a UI component." turn[c("stop", "generated_tokens")] #> $stop #> [1] "max_new" #>  #> $generated_tokens #> [1] 12

title: Events and adapters
text: named list can also supplied directly. Unknown handler names rejected stale event code fails early.
code: counts <- c(text_delta = 0L, thinking_delta = 0L, done = 0L)  invisible(bebel_generate(   model,   "An event handler list can",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 4,   max_think = 16,   on_event = list(     text_delta = function(event) counts["text_delta"] <<- counts[["text_delta"]] + 1L,     thinking_delta = function(event) counts["thinking_delta"] <<- counts[["thinking_delta"]] + 1L,     done = function(event) counts["done"] <<- counts[["done"]] + 1L   ) ))  counts #>     text_delta thinking_delta           done  #>              4              0              1

title: Events and adapters
text: SSE endpoint can serialize event arrives. sketch shows adapter shape; exact response object depends web framework. Tool adapters use event stream. bebel_agent_run() listens tool_call_end events, parses accumulated content, invokes R tools, appends tool results transcript.
code:
sse_handler <- function(response) {   bebel_event_handler(     default = function(event) {       payload <- jsonlite::toJSON(event, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null")       response$write(paste0("event: ", event$type, "
"))       response$write(paste0("data: ", payload, "

"))       response$flush()     }   ) }  bebel_agent_generate(agent, on_event = sse_handler(response))

title: Getting started
text: BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE set, use code explicit file path:
code: model <- bebel_model_load(Sys.getenv("BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE"), num_threads = 2) rbebelm_backend_features()[c("backend", "target_arch", "target_os")] #> $backend #> [1] "avx2" #>  #> $target_arch #> [1] "x86_64" #>  #> $target_os #> [1] "linux" model <- bebel_model_load("LFM2.5-8B-A1B-Q4_K_M.gguf", num_threads = 2)

title: Getting started
text: main interface BebelAgent. agent owns transcript decode caches sharing loaded model weights. Use bebel_clear(agent) reset transcript caches without reloading model.
code:
agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 48, max_think = 16)  bebel_append_user(agent, "What is the capital of France? Answer briefly.") turn1 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  bebel_append_user(agent, "And Italy?") turn2 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  turn1$text #> [1] "<think>
The user asks: "What is the capital of France? Answer briefly."</think>
The capital of France is Paris." turn2$text #> [1] "<think>
The user asks: "And Italy?" Possibly they are continuing a conversation</think>
The capital of Italy is Rome." bebel_agent_info(agent)[c("history_tokens", "processed_tokens", "kv_tokens")] #> $history_tokens #> [1] 86 #>  #> $processed_tokens #> [1] 84 #>  #> $kv_tokens #> [1] 84

title: Getting started
text: simple calls, bebel_chat() creates single ChatML user/assistant turn. raw prompt completion, use bebel_generate().
code: chat <- bebel_chat(   model,   "In one concise sentence, what does runtime backend dispatch do?",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 48,   max_think = 16,   on_event = NULL ) chat$text #> [1] "<think>\nThe user asks: \"In one concise sentence, what does runtime backend dispatch</think>\nRuntime backend dispatch assigns incoming requests to the appropriate service or function based on dynamic criteria at execution time. That's one sentence. But they want \""

title: Getting started
code: ids <- bebel_tokenize(model, "The capital of Italy is", add_bos = TRUE) ids #> [1] 124894    597   5205    302  10125    355 bebel_detokenize(model, ids) #> [1] "<|startoftext|>The capital of Italy is" bebel_token_ids()[c("TOKEN_THINK", "TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_START", "TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_END")] #>           TOKEN_THINK TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_START   TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_END  #>                124901                124905                124906

title: Authors and Citation
text: Sounkou Mahamane Toure. Author, maintainer. Maxime Chevalier-Boisvert. Copyright holder.           BebeLM upstream Hiroaki Yutani. Copyright holder.           savvy R/Rust interface

text: Toure S (2026). Rbebelm: 'R' Bindings 'BebeLM' 'CPU' Inference. R package version 0.2.0-0.1.0, https://github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/Rbebelm.
code: @Manual{,   title = {Rbebelm: 'R' Bindings for 'BebeLM' 'CPU' Inference},   author = {Sounkou Mahamane Toure},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0-0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/Rbebelm}, }

what: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Rbebelm provides experimental R bindings upstream maximecb/bebelm, pure-Rust CPU-implementation Liquid AI LFM2.5-8B-A1B inference. R package uses savvy R/Rust boundary Rsassy-style runtime backend layout portable SIMD dispatch. package designed interactive LLM use: generation streams tokens R console soon decoded, function still returns final text, token ids, stop reason, timing statistics.

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Install R-universe: R-universe can also publish Linux binaries universe. prefer binaries Linux, use universe binary repository pattern: Source installs require Cargo/rustc GNU make. Linux, macOS, Windows, Rbebelm builds separate Rust backend libraries possible: scalar, AVX2, AVX-512 x86_64; scalar NEON arm64. dispatcher selects best installed backend supported current CPU/runtime loading model code. model weights bundled R package. Download GGUF weights upstream model source documented bebelm, pass local path bebel_model_load().
code: install.packages(   "Rbebelm",   repos = c("https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) options(repos = c(   Rbebelm = sprintf(     "https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev/bin/linux/noble-%s/%s/",     R.version$arch,     substr(getRversion(), 1, 3)   ),   CRAN = sprintf(     "https://cran.r-universe.dev/bin/linux/noble-%s/%s/",     R.version$arch,     substr(getRversion(), 1, 3)   ) )) install.packages("Rbebelm")

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Set BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE local GGUF path, replace weights explicit file path. README examples evaluated local model file available rendering. BebelAgent owns token transcript decode caches sharing loaded model weights. Later turns prefill newly appended tokens. Use bebel_clear(agent) reset transcript caches without reloading GGUF. ellmer-style terminal loop, call bebel_live_console(agent) bebel_live_console(model) interactive R session. can also create console directly model: Convenience helpers still available simple calls. bebel_chat() wraps single ChatML user/assistant turn: plain text completion, use bebel_generate(): Agents can also driven lower level raw text token ids: Tools can orchestrated Agent run loop. context object private R tools hooks; sent model. tool dispatched model emits BebeLM tool-call block, prompts describe available tools expected call format. prompt asks directly tool-call form example exercises dispatch path. event stream can consumed programmatically. example, collect answer-text deltas suppressing console output: can also pass named list event-specific handlers directly:
code: library(Rbebelm)  weights <- Sys.getenv("BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE", "LFM2.5-8B-A1B-Q4_K_M.gguf") model <- bebel_model_load(weights, num_threads = 2)  # Agent-first API: one loaded model can back several conversations. agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 48, max_think = 16)  bebel_append_user(agent, "What is the capital of France? Answer briefly.") turn1 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  bebel_append_user(agent, "And Italy?") turn2 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  turn1 #> <BebeLM assistant turn> #>   stop: eos  #>   tokens: 26 generated; 19 prompt #>   prefill: 9.6 tok/s  #>   decode: 9.67 tok/s  #>   text: #> <think> #> The user asks: "What is the capital of France? Answer briefly."</think> #> The capital of France is Paris. turn2 #> <BebeLM assistant turn> #>   stop: eos  #>   tokens: 26 generated; 13 prompt #>   prefill: 9.8 tok/s  #>   decode: 9.80 tok/s  #>   text: #> <think> #> The user asks: "And Italy?" Possibly they are continuing a conversation</think> #> The capital of Italy is Rome. bebel_agent_info(agent)[c("history_tokens", "processed_tokens", "kv_tokens")] #> $history_tokens #> [1] 86 #>  #> $processed_tokens #> [1] 84 #>  #> $kv_tokens #> [1] 84 chat <- bebel_agent(model, max_gen = 256, max_think = 64) bebel_live_console(chat) #> ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ #> ║  Entering BebeLM live console.                     ║ #> ║  Type /quit or /exit to return to R.               ║ #> ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ #> >>> What is BebeLM? #> <think> #> The user asks for a brief explanation of BebeLM. #> </think> #> BebeLM is a small, CPU-focused local language model runtime written in Rust. #> #> >>> Why use the R agent API? #> The R agent keeps the transcript and decode caches alive across turns while #> sharing the loaded GGUF weights. #> #> >>> /quit bebel_live_console(model, max_gen = 256, max_think = 64) # on_event defaults to bebel_console_event(): thinking and text print live. result <- bebel_chat(   model,   "In one concise sentence, what does runtime SIMD dispatch do?",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 48,   max_think = 16,   on_event = bebel_console_event(),   check_interrupt = TRUE ) #> <think> #> The user asks: "In one concise sentence, what does runtime SIMD</think> #> Runtime SIMD dispatch dynamically selects and executes the most efficient instruction variant for the current hardware at execution time, allowing programs to adapt to varying processor  result #> <BebeLM chat result> #>   stop: max_new  #>   tokens: 48 generated; 22 prompt #>   prefill: 9.7 tok/s  #>   decode: 9.80 tok/s  #>   text: #> <think> #> The user asks: "In one concise sentence, what does runtime SIMD</think> #> Runtime SIMD dispatch dynamically selects and executes the most efficient instruction variant for the current hardware at execution time, allowing programs to adapt to varying processor raw_result <- bebel_generate(   model,   "Runtime SIMD dispatch is useful because",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 24,   max_think = 16,   on_event = bebel_console_event(),   check_interrupt = TRUE ) #>  it allows the compiler to generate code that is specific to the target processor architecture, which can lead to better performance. However raw_result #> <BebeLM generation result> #>   stop: max_new  #>   tokens: 24 generated; 8 prompt #>   prefill: 9.7 tok/s  #>   decode: 10.15 tok/s  #>   text: #>  it allows the compiler to generate code that is specific to the target processor architecture, which can lead to better performance. However raw_agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 16, max_think = 0) bebel_append(raw_agent, "The capital of France is") raw_turn <- bebel_agent_generate(raw_agent, on_event = NULL)  ids <- bebel_tokenize(model, " and its airport code is", add_bos = FALSE) bebel_append_tokens(raw_agent, ids) bebel_history(raw_agent)[1:8] #> [1] 124894    597   5205    302   3980    355   4741     22 bebel_token_ids()[c("TOKEN_THINK", "TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_START", "TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_END")] #>           TOKEN_THINK TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_START   TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_END  #>                124901                124905                124906 raw_turn$text #> [1] " Paris. city name: france. name: france. city name: paris." ctx <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) ctx$thread_id <- "thread-001" ctx$log <- character()  tools <- list(   lookup_capital = bebel_tool(     "lookup_capital",     function(args, context, call) {       context$log <- c(context$log, paste("tool", call$name, args$country))       c(France = "Paris", Italy = "Rome")[[args$country]]     },     description = "Return a capital city for a country."   ) )  hooks <- list(   tool_request = function(call, context, ...) {     context$log <- c(context$log, paste("request", call$name))   },   tool_result = function(call, result, context, ...) {     context$log <- c(context$log, paste("result", call$name, result))   } )  tool_prompt <- paste(   "Return exactly this tool call and no other text:",   "lookup_capital({\"country\":\"Italy\"})" )  agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 64, max_think = 0) bebel_append_user(agent, tool_prompt) run <- bebel_agent_run(agent, tools = tools, context = ctx, hooks = hooks, max_steps = 2) run #> <bebelAgentRun> #>   turns: 2  #>   tool calls: 1  #> <BebeLM assistant turn> #>   stop: eos  #>   tokens: 34 generated; 31 prompt #>   prefill: 9.9 tok/s  #>   decode: 9.77 tok/s  #>   text: #> { #>   "tool_call": { #>     "name": "lookup_capital", #>     "parameters": { #>       "country": "Italy" #>     } #>   } #> } ctx$log #> [1] "request lookup_capital"     "tool lookup_capital Italy"  #> [3] "result lookup_capital Rome" deltas <- character() invisible(bebel_generate(   model,   "A text delta callback can",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 12,   max_think = 16,   on_event = bebel_event_handler(     text_delta = function(event) deltas <<- c(deltas, event$delta)   ) )) paste0(deltas, collapse = "") #> [1] " be used to update a text field in a UI component." counts <- c(text_delta = 0L, thinking_delta = 0L, done = 0L) invisible(bebel_generate(   model,   "An event handler list can",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 4,   max_think = 16,   on_event = list(     text_delta = function(event) counts["text_delta"] <<- counts[["text_delta"]] + 1L,     thinking_delta = function(event) counts["thinking_delta"] <<- counts[["thinking_delta"]] + 1L,     done = function(event) counts["done"] <<- counts[["done"]] + 1L   ) )) counts #>     text_delta thinking_delta           done  #>              4              0              1

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Generation checks R_CheckUserInterrupt() prompt prefill every decoded token, wrapped savvy’s unwind protection Ctrl-C longjmp Rust frames. Streaming event-based uses finite event protocol; bebel_event_types() reports event enum build. current enum returned bebel_event_types() includes stream lifecycle, thinking blocks, answer text blocks, tool-list blocks, tool-call blocks, done. Delta events contain delta, id, index; control start/end events include delimiter token id marker; end events contain accumulated content. Console printing just default event handler. Use on_event = NULL silent batch-style generation.

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Rbebelm builds real Rust/savvy backend webR static wasm_simd128 backend. wasm build uses patched local copy upstream BebeLM avoids native-mmap Rayon imports Emscripten: GGUF files read webR filesystem memory matmul runs serially. mount download GGUF webR virtual filesystem, bebel_model_load() attempt load . large models can still exhaust browser/webR memory.

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Rbebelm installs one small R shared library plus separate Rust backend libraries. R shared library owns registration dispatch; model code lives selected backend library. dispatcher checks CPU/OS support loading SIMD backends, portable binary can avoid executing unsupported instructions. Backend selection happens per R process. need benchmark debug specific backend, call rbebelm_set_backend() first native Rbebelm call fresh Rscript process: Inspect current CPU/runtime selected backend:
code: rbebelm_set_backend("auto") rbebelm_cpuid_info() #> <Rbebelm CPU features> #>   x86_64-v3: yes  #>   x86_64-v4: no  #>   NEON: no  #>   wasm simd128: no rbebelm_backend_features() #> <Rbebelm backend features> #>   backend: avx2  #>   target: x86_64-linux  #>   Rust crate: rbebelm_backend 0.1.0  #>   native SIMD feature: yes  #>   compiled features: #>     AVX2: yes  #>     AVX-512F: no  #>     NEON: no  #>     wasm simd128: no rbebelm_backend_info() #> <Rbebelm backend dispatch> #>   mode: dynamic  #>   requested: auto  #>   selected: avx2  #>   loaded: yes  #>   installed: scalar,avx2,avx512  #>   supported: scalar,avx2

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Common development commands repository root. make vignettes target uses rawvignette; install remotes::install_github("matthewkling/rawvignette") editing vignettes-raw/.
code: make rd           # regenerate savvy wrappers, dispatch init, NAMESPACE, and man/*.Rd make rdm          # regenerate README.md from evaluated README.Rmd make dev-install  # install the package locally from source make test         # run tinytest tests make check        # build and run R CMD check --no-manual make vignettes    # precompile vignettes-raw/ into vignettes/ with rawvignette make site         # build the pkgdown site make clean        # remove generated build artifacts

what: Run a BebeLM agent with R tool dispatch — bebel_agent_run
title: Run a BebeLM agent with R tool dispatch — bebel_agent_run
text: Agent-first orchestration loop. observes tool_call_end events, parses tool calls, invokes matching R tools private context, appends tool results agent transcript, continues generation.

title: Run a BebeLM agent with R tool dispatch — bebel_agent_run
code: bebel_agent_run(   agent,   tools = list(),   context = new.env(parent = emptyenv()),   hooks = list(),   parse_tool_call = bebel_parse_tool_call,   max_steps = 4,   on_event = NULL,   check_interrupt = TRUE )

title: Run a BebeLM agent with R tool dispatch — bebel_agent_run
text: agent BebelAgent object. tools list bebel_tool() objects named functions. context Private run context passed tools hooks appended model transcript. hooks Optional named list hooks: turn_start, event, tool_request, tool_result, tool_error, turn_end. parse_tool_call Function converting tool-call content list(name, arguments, raw). max_steps Maximum assistant/tool iterations. on_event Optional event callback handler list model events. check_interrupt Check Ctrl-C generation.
